question.text: What is 'option premium erosion' (time decay) behavior for ITM vs OTM options?

answer.text: OTM options lose their entire value to time decay since they have no intrinsic value. ITM options lose only their time value component — the intrinsic value portion is preserved. ATM options experience the highest absolute time decay
ITM options have the fastest time decay because they are the most expensive
OTM options have no time decay because they have no value to lose
All options decay at the same rate regardless of moneyness

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Time decay affects only the time value component: ATM options have the most time value → most absolute theta. OTM options have modest time value → less theta but 100% of their value is subject to decay. ITM options: only the time value portion decays; intrinsic is preserved. **The Trap:** OTM options DO have time value (their entire premium is time value). ITM options are expensive but have less time value (more intrinsic). Decay rates differ by moneyness. **Cross-Exam Context:** Time decay behavior is universal across all option markets. Understanding this helps select the right strikes for different strategies. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Option sellers prefer ATM (highest absolute decay) for income generation, while buyers prefer OTM (cheaper, higher leverage) for directional bets.
